title: Synthomer Partners with Godavari Biorefineries Limited to Develop Bio-Based Alternatives to Fossil-Based Monomers
content: Synthomer announced a new partnership with Godavari Biorefineries Limited (GBL) to develop bioâ€‘based alternatives to existing fossilâ€‘based monomers. With growing customer demand for more sustainable alternatives, this partnership strengthens Synthomerâ€™s commitment to innovation and reinforces our focus on delivering customerâ€‘centric, lowerâ€‘carbon solutions. Through this partnership, Synthomer is commercialising bio-based butyl acrylate using bio-based butanol produced by GBL, helping to accelerate the industryâ€™s transition to more sustainable raw materials. Synthomer has officially joined forces with Godavari Biorefineries Limited in an exciting new partnership aimed at developing bio based alternatives to traditional fossil based monomers. This strategic collaboration focuses on leveraging Godavariâ€™s expertise in biorefining and sustainable agricultural feedstocks to create high performance chemical building blocks. By integrating these bio based materials into Synthomerâ€™s diverse product portfolio, the companies are accelerating the shift toward more sustainable chemistry in the specialty chemicals sector. This move is specifically designed to reduce the carbon footprint of essential products while maintaining the high standards of quality and functionality that industrial customers expect. The partnership represents a significant step in the global journey toward a circular economy, as it prioritizes renewable resources over depleting fossil fuels. Together, Synthomer and Godavari Biorefineries are driving innovation that meets the growing market demand for environmentally friendly solutions. This initiative not only strengthens the supply chain for sustainable raw materials but also demonstrates a shared commitment to long term ecological responsibility.
